nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.seed_question: Hi, I'm Kandidly and I'll be running your screening interview today. It'll take up to about thirty minutes, and I'll ask follow-up questions as we go — that's completely normal. To start, could you tell me a little about your background and what you're working on right now?

nodes.title: Recent project deep-dive
nodes.seed_question: Tell me about a backend system you built or owned recently. What was the problem it solved, and what were you personally responsible for?

nodes.title: System design & tradeoffs
nodes.seed_question: Walk me through how you'd design a service that needs to handle a large volume of writes reliably. What are the main components and the tradeoffs you'd weigh?

nodes.title: Data & consistency
nodes.seed_question: How do you think about data consistency and failure handling in a distributed system you've worked on? Give me a concrete example.

nodes.title: Debugging a hard problem
nodes.seed_question: Describe the hardest production issue you've debugged. How did you approach it, and what did you learn?

nodes.title: Testing & quality
nodes.seed_question: How do you decide what to test and how much? Tell me how you've balanced speed and reliability in practice.

nodes.title: Collaboration & ownership
nodes.seed_question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a technical decision on your team. How did you handle it?

nodes.title: Your questions
nodes.seed_question: That's everything from my side. Is there anything you'd like to ask about the role or the team?

nodes.title: Wrap up
nodes.seed_question: Thank you so much for your time today — the team will review and be in touch with next steps. Take care!
